concepts.lesson: Social justice means ensuring fairness and equality in society so that all people have equal opportunities and rights. It aims to protect weaker sections of society and prevent exploitation. Laws are created to ensure that workers, consumers and communities are treated fairly.
concepts.real_example: Laws protecting workers from being paid extremely low wages.

concepts.concept_name: Why Laws are Needed for Social Justice
concepts.lesson: Laws are necessary to prevent exploitation and unfair practices in society. Without laws, powerful individuals or companies might take advantage of workers or consumers. Laws ensure that people receive fair treatment and that their rights are protected.
concepts.real_example: Rules that require factories to maintain safe working conditions for workers.

concepts.lesson: The minimum wage law ensures that workers receive at least a basic level of payment for their work. This law prevents employers from paying extremely low wages to workers who depend on their jobs for survival. Minimum wages are set by the government and are revised from time to time.
concepts.real_example: Construction workers receiving a fixed minimum wage set by the government.

concepts.lesson: Workplace safety laws require employers to provide safe working environments. These laws ensure that factories and workplaces maintain safety equipment, proper machinery and emergency systems. Such regulations protect workers from accidents and health risks.
concepts.real_example: Factories installing fire alarms and safety exits to protect workers.

concepts.concept_name: Role of Government in Enforcing Laws
concepts.lesson: The government plays an important role in enforcing laws related to social justice. It monitors workplaces, investigates violations and punishes those who break the law. Enforcement is important because simply creating laws is not enough; they must be properly implemented.
concepts.real_example: Government inspectors checking factories to ensure workers receive minimum wages.

concepts.lesson: Laws also protect consumers from unsafe or poor-quality products. These laws require companies to follow safety standards and ensure that products do not harm people. Consumer protection laws help maintain trust between producers and buyers.
concepts.real_example: Food safety rules that prevent the sale of harmful or contaminated food.

concepts.lesson: Environmental protection laws prevent industries from polluting air, water and land. These laws are important for protecting natural resources and ensuring healthy living conditions for communities. Companies must follow environmental regulations while carrying out industrial activities.
concepts.real_example: Factories required to treat waste before releasing it into rivers.

concepts.concept_name: Importance of Enforcing Laws for Justice
concepts.lesson: Even when laws exist, they are effective only if they are properly enforced. If laws are ignored or weakly implemented, workers and communities may continue to face injustice. Therefore, strong enforcement and public awareness are essential to achieve social justice.
concepts.real_example: Strict monitoring of factories to ensure they follow labour and safety laws.
